manifest_notes: Authentication and account setup problem where email appears unset.

issue.title: Email shows as not set

labels.name: area/abctl
labels.description: Issues with the abctl quickstart cli

issue.body:
### What happened? When running abctl local credentials, the email is marked as: ### What did you expect to happen? When I go to older versions of airbyte, the email field is shown correctly when running this command ### Abctl Version
```console $ abctl version version: v0.30.1 ```
### Docker Version
```console $ docker version Client: Docker Engine - Community Version: 28.3.3 API version: 1.51 Go version: go1.24.5 Git commit: 980b856 Built: Fri Jul 25 11:34:00 2025 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 Context: default Server: Docker Engine - Community Engine: Version: 28.3.3 API version: 1.51 (minimum version 1.24) Go version: go1.24.5 Git commit: bea959c Built: Fri Jul 25 11:34:00 2025 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 Experimental: false containerd: Version: 1.7.27 GitCommit: 05044ec0a9a75232cad458027ca83437aae3f4da runc: Version: 1.2.5 GitCommit: v1.2.5-0-g59923ef docker-init: Version: 0.19.0 GitCommit: de40ad0 ```
### OS Version
```console # On Linux: $ cat /etc/os-release PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)" NAME="Debian GNU/Linux" VERSION_ID="12" VERSION="12 (bookworm)" VERSION_CODENAME=bookworm ID=debian HOME_URL="https://www.debian.org/" SUPPORT_URL="https://www.debian.org/support" BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.debian.org/" ```

comments.body: I got same issue root@test-ldap-ansible-29:/etc/nginx/certs# abctl local status INFO Using Kubernetes provider: Provider: kind Kubeconfig: /root/.airbyte/abctl/abctl.kubeconfig Context: kind-airbyte-abctl SUCCESS Found Docker installation: version 28.3.3 SUCCESS Existing cluster 'airbyte-abctl' found INFO Found helm chart 'airbyte-abctl' Status: failed Chart Version: 2.0.12 App Version: 1.8.2 INFO Found helm chart 'ingress-nginx' Status: deployed Chart Version: 4.13.2 App Version: 1.13.2 INFO Airbyte should be accessible via http://localhost:8000
Same issue here, unable to update or set it, and unable to login without a username

I'm also facing the same issue, with abctl version v0.30.1.

`usr:~$ abctl local credentials
  INFO    Using Kubernetes provider:
            Provider: kind
            Kubeconfig: /home/dilanalex/.airbyte/abctl/abctl.kubeconfig
            Context: kind-airbyte-abctl
 SUCCESS  Retrieving your credentials from 'airbyte-auth-secrets'
  INFO    Credentials:
            Email: [not set]
            Password: B5GR3LtbbcNTdIWigxxhf9M3HkGlIZ0i
            Client-Id: ddb16e54-2207-412a-8ea6-91708abffc56
            Client-Secret: JNnUgREAFJshRkZAZIf3q7muHPrkcebd`

When updating email address,

`usr:~$ abctl local credentials --email dilan.silva@mycompany.com
  INFO    Using Kubernetes provider:
            Provider: kind
            Kubeconfig: /home/dilanalex/.airbyte/abctl/abctl.kubeconfig
            Context: kind-airbyte-abctl
  INFO    Updating email for authentication
  ERROR   Unable to update the email address
  ERROR   unable to udpate the email address: failed to update organization: unexpected status code: 401`

also facing the same error:

  INFO    Using Kubernetes provider:
            Provider: kind
            Kubeconfig: /home/svc_airbyte/.airbyte/abctl/abctl.kubeconfig
            Context: kind-airbyte-abctl
 SUCCESS  Found Docker installation: version 27.3.1
 SUCCESS  Existing cluster 'airbyte-abctl' found
  INFO    Found helm chart 'airbyte-abctl'
            Status: deployed
            Chart Version: 2.0.17
            App Version: 1.8.5
  INFO    Found helm chart 'ingress-nginx'
            Status: deployed
            Chart Version: 4.13.3
            App Version: 1.13.3
  INFO    Airbyte should be accessible via http://localhost:8000

Error during credential update.
  Unable to update the email address
  ERROR   unable to udpate the email address: failed to update organization: unexpected status code: 401
Same as above. Is there a way to self correct ?(for example, by manually updating the database)

I've just tested this on the current dev version of abctl with the latest chart and platform version (2.0), and it does appear to be functioning normally.  I can set my email both in the CLI and the web app, and retrieve it as expected.

We're working to get some releases out ASAP and are doing final testing and fixes. Shouldn't be long. Thanks for your patience.
The new version (2.0) is up. Please upgrade to the latest version of abctl and Airbyte and then give it a try when you can.

Same issue here - email was not set, and running the command with -`-email` to set it returned a 401 error.
I upgraded to the new version (2.0.0), and setting the email works now.
Still keeping an eye out for the root cause and any manual fix for version 1.8.5 (Helm chart v2.0.17).

I do see an improvement with the latest build.

Installed `abctl` through
`curl -LsfS https://get.airbyte.com | bash -`

Setup the local cluster with
`abctl local install`

A browser opens to collect the email. Note: running `abctl local credentials` before clicking "Get Started" will return [not set] for email. The email is set after "Get Started" is clicked
I usually need to run `abctl local credentials` with sudo to get the password
